dialogue.speaker: cupbearer
dialogue.englishText: My sins I remember today

dialogue.speaker: cupbearer
dialogue.englishText: Pharaoh was angry with his servants and put me in custody in the house of the captain of the guard, me and the chief baker

dialogue.speaker: cupbearer
dialogue.englishText: And we dreamed a dream in the same night, he and I, each according to the interpretation of his dream we dreamed

dialogue.speaker: cupbearer
dialogue.englishText: And there was with us a young man, a Hebrew, a servant of the captain of the guard; and we told him, and he interpreted for us our dreams, to each man according to his dream he interpreted

dialogue.speaker: cupbearer
dialogue.englishText: And it came to pass, as he interpreted to us, so it was: me he restored to my position, and him he hanged

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: I have dreamed a dream, and there is no one who can interpret it; and I have heard it said of you that when you hear a dream you can interpret it

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Not I; God will give Pharaoh a favorable answer

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: In my dream, behold, I was standing on the bank of the Nile

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: And behold, out of the Nile came up seven cows, fat and attractive, and they grazed in the reed grass

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: And behold, seven other cows came up after them, poor and very ugly and thin, such as I had never seen in all the land of Egypt for ugliness

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: And the thin and ugly cows ate up the first seven fat cows

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: But when they had eaten them no one would have known that they had eaten them, for they were still as ugly as at the beginning; then I awoke

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: I also saw in my dream seven ears growing on one stalk, full and good

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: And behold, seven ears, withered, thin, blighted by the east wind, sprouting after them

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: And the thin ears swallowed up the seven good ears; and I told it to the magicians, but there was no one who could explain it to me

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Pharaoh's dream is one; what God is about to do he has revealed to Pharaoh

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: The seven good cows are seven years, and the seven good ears are seven years; the dream is one

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: And the seven lean and ugly cows that came up after them are seven years, and the seven empty ears blighted by the east wind will be seven years of famine

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: This is the thing I told Pharaoh: what God is about to do he has shown to Pharaoh

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Behold, seven years of great plenty are coming throughout all the land of Egypt

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: But after them there will arise seven years of famine, and all the plenty will be forgotten in the land of Egypt; the famine will consume the land

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: And the plenty will be unknown in the land by reason of the famine that will follow, for it will be very severe

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: And the doubling of Pharaoh's dream means that the thing is fixed by God, and God will shortly bring it about

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Now therefore let Pharaoh select a discerning and wise man, and set him over the land of Egypt

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Let Pharaoh proceed to appoint overseers over the land and take one-fifth of the produce of the land of Egypt during the seven plentiful years

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: And let them gather all the food of these good years that are coming and store up grain under the authority of Pharaoh for food in the cities, and let them keep it

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: That food shall be a reserve for the land against the seven years of famine that are to occur in the land of Egypt, so that the land may not perish through the famine

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: Can we find a man like this, in whom is the Spirit of God?

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: Since God has shown you all this, there is none so discerning and wise as you are

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: You shall be over my house, and all my people shall order themselves as you command; only as regards the throne will I be greater than you

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: See, I have set you over all the land of Egypt

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: I am Pharaoh, and without your consent no man shall lift up hand or foot in all the land of Egypt

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: Go to Joseph. What he says to you, do
